bundle_description: How Mirofish spawns and coordinates sub-agents — from task decomposition through parallel execution to result aggregation.

features.name: Root Agent Orchestration
features.description: Top-level orchestrator that receives a complex task, decomposes it into subtasks, and manages the lifecycle of sub-agents.

features.name: Sub-Agent Spawning
features.description: Dynamic agent pool that instantiates specialized sub-agents with isolated contexts, tool sets, and system prompts.

features.name: Task Decomposition
features.description: LLM-powered task planner that breaks complex goals into a dependency-aware DAG of atomic subtasks.

features.name: Result Aggregation
features.description: Collects outputs from completed sub-agents, resolves conflicts, merges partial results, and synthesizes a final answer.

features.name: Error Recovery & Retry
features.description: Handles sub-agent failures with configurable retry policies, fallback agents, and partial-result salvage strategies.

call_chain.role: Main entry point that accepts a user goal, runs task decomposition, spawns sub-agents, waits for results, and aggregates the final answer.
Analyzes the task graph to determine execution order, identifying which subtasks can run in parallel and which have dependencies.
Dispatches a batch of independent subtasks concurrently using asyncio.gather, collecting results as they complete.
After each wave completes, checks if all subtasks are done or if there are remaining tasks whose dependencies are now satisfied.
Takes all sub-agent results and produces a coherent final answer by calling the LLM with a synthesis prompt.

root_orchestration.explanation: The root orchestrator implements a wave-based execution model. orchestrate() first calls TaskGraph.decompose() to break the user's goal into subtasks with dependency edges. Then _plan_execution() performs a topological sort on the task DAG to identify execution waves — groups of tasks with no unmet dependencies. _dispatch_wave() launches all tasks in the current wave concurrently using asyncio.gather(), with each task handled by a spawned sub-agent. After each wave, _check_completion() verifies results and unlocks the next wave of dependent tasks. This continues until all tasks complete. Finally, _synthesize_answer() sends all sub-agent outputs to the LLM to produce a single coherent response.

call_chain.role: Creates a new sub-agent instance with an isolated execution context, assigns tools and system prompt based on the subtask type.
Chooses the agent profile (researcher, coder, analyst, writer) based on the subtask's classified type and required capabilities.
Initializes the sub-agent with its profile's system prompt, tool registry, context window budget, and execution timeout.
Runs the sub-agent's ReAct loop for its assigned subtask — prompt construction, LLM call, tool use, iterating until completion.
Creates a sandboxed context with its own memory, tool results, and token budget that doesn't leak into other agents.

sub_agent_spawning.explanation: Sub-agent spawning follows a profile-based architecture. When SubAgentPool.spawn() is called with a subtask, _select_profile() classifies the task and selects an appropriate agent profile — 'researcher' for information gathering, 'coder' for code generation, 'analyst' for data analysis, etc. Each profile defines a system prompt, a set of available tools, and resource limits. AgentContext.create_isolated() builds a sandboxed execution environment so sub-agents can't see each other's intermediate results. SubAgent.__init__() wires everything together, and SubAgent.execute() runs the actual ReAct loop — constructing prompts, calling the LLM, parsing tool use, and iterating until the subtask is complete or the timeout is reached.

call_chain.role: Takes a complex user goal and calls the LLM to produce a structured list of subtasks with dependency relationships.
Parses the LLM's JSON response into SubTask objects with ids, descriptions, dependency lists, and type classifications.
Constructs a directed acyclic graph from the subtask dependency lists, validating for cycles and unreachable nodes.
Runs validation checks on the DAG — ensures no cycles, all dependencies exist, root tasks have no dependencies, and leaf tasks produce outputs.
Returns all subtasks whose dependencies are fully satisfied and are ready for execution in the current wave.

task_decomposition.explanation: Task decomposition uses an LLM to convert high-level goals into executable plans. decompose() sends the user's goal to the LLM with a structured prompt requesting a JSON plan with subtasks and dependencies. _parse_plan() validates and deserializes the response into SubTask objects, each with an ID, description, type (research/code/analysis/write), and a list of dependency IDs. _build_dag() constructs a networkx DiGraph from these relationships. _validate() checks for cycles (which would cause deadlocks), orphan tasks, and ensures the graph has clear entry points. get_ready_tasks() is called during execution to find which tasks can be dispatched next — those whose dependencies are all marked complete.
